ddls:
CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "sample_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the dataset in bytes */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was uploaded */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the dataset (e.g., financial, scientific) */,
  "labels" TEXT /* Labels associated with the dataset (e.g., regression, classification) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data in the dataset (e.g., numerical, categorical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_category FOREIGN KEY ("category") REFERENCES dataset_categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_labels FOREIGN KEY ("labels") REFERENCES dataset_labels ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sorting_algorithms" (
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sorting algorithm */,
  "algorithm_name" TEXT /* Name of the sorting algorithm (e.g., Insertion Sort) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the algorithm */,
  "time_complexity" TEXT /* Time complexity of the algorithm (e.g., O(n^2), O(n log n)) */,
  "space_complexity" TEXT /* Space complexity of the algorithm (e.g., O(1), O(n)) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("algorithm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "benchmark_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benchmark result */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for testing */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sorting algorithm applied */,
  "sort_time" REAL /* Time taken for sorting (in seconds) */,
  "compares" INTEGER /* Number of comparisons made during sorting */,
  "mem_access" INTEGER /* Memory accesses during sorting */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date of the benchmark test */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was run (e.g., local, cloud) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed for security) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user accessed the platform */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view dataset, run benchmark) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from which the user accessed the platform (e.g., IP address) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the notification is for */,
  "message" TEXT /* Notification message content */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the request */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint being accessed */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Time the request was made */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Time the response was received */,
  "request_method" TEXT /* HTTP method used for the request (e.g., GET, POST) */,
  "response_status" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who encountered the error */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the error occurred */,
  "error_level" TEXT /* Level of the error (e.g., debug, info, warning, error) */,
  "error_stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benchmark result this metric relates to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., seconds, milliseconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test parameter */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the benchmark result this parameter relates to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the test parameter (e.g., dataset size) */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the test parameter */,
  "parameter_type" TEXT /* Type of the parameter (e.g., integer, string) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_uploads" (
  "upload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset upload */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user uploading the dataset */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being uploaded */,
  "upload_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the dataset was uploaded */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the uploaded file in MB */,
  "upload_status" TEXT /* Status of the upload (e.g., pending, complete, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("upload_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_labels" (
  "label_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each label */,
  "label_name" TEXT /* Name of the label */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the label */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("label_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "related_dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related dataset */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., identical, similar, different) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_relationships_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_relationships_related_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("related_dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)
